items.title: Spot Prices Wrap: Softening Demand Pressures Natural Gas as Northeast Losses Outpace West Texas Gains
items.summary: Cash markets posted widespread losses for Wednesday delivery as softer weather-driven demand weighed on most regions, though gains at West Texas hubs helped limit broader declines.

items.title: Futures Settle: Front-End Strength, Back-End Weakness Define April Natural Gas Trading
items.summary: Natural gas futures were higher Tuesday at the front of the curve as rebounding oil prices, lingering domestic demand, and expectations for at least one more storage withdrawal lent support. Meanwhile, longer-dated contracts weakened on optimism that

items.title: Natural Gas ‘Headwinds Have Become Tailwinds’ Amid Iran War, Says ConocoPhillips CEO
items.summary: Whether the war with Iran comes to a swift end, a lift in global natural gas futures is likely locked in with limited production on the horizon, according to ConocoPhillips CEO Ryan Lance.

items.title: MidDay Snapshot: Warmer Weather Pressures Natural Gas Cash Prices
items.summary: Natural gas spot prices fell across most of the country through midday Tuesday as warming weather pressured demand.

items.title: California Shrugs Off Reduced Natural Gas Flows as Negative Permian Prices Persist
items.summary: There have been only 10 days so far this year in which Permian Basin benchmark Waha has traded above zero, highlighting the disconnect between surging global natural gas prices amid the Iran war and domestic oversupply in one of the Lower 48’s most p

items.title: Venture Global Leans on Flexible Contracts to Drive 40 Mt LNG Expansion
items.summary: In the wake of ratcheting conflict in the Middle East and global volatility, Venture Global Inc. is counting on price stability and its flexible contracts to fuel its next round of brownfield expansions in Louisiana.

items.title: MidDay Futures: Natural Gas Edges Higher as Middle East Tensions Outweigh Milder Weather Outlook
items.summary: Natural gas futures rose a few cents on Tuesday morning as hopes for a ceasefire in the Middle East dimmed, adding room for more disruption to global energy flows.

items.title: Midmorning Markets: Ample Supply Limiting War Risk Premium for U.S. Natural Gas
items.summary: Soft shoulder season fundamentals are pressuring U.S. natural gas futures and cash prices to the downside and limiting volatility, despite turmoil in global energy markets as the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran nears its one-month anniversary.

items.title: Double E Gauging Shipper Interest in Permian Natural Gas Pipeline Expansion
items.summary: Double E Pipeline LLC is seeking long-term shipper commitments for a proposed expansion of its interstate pipeline system, which pulls gas from the Permian Basin in West Texas and southeastern New Mexico.

items.title: Market Open: Natural Gas Futures Creeping Higher Amid Iran Uncertainty, Looming Expiry
items.summary: Having shed 27.5 cents over the prior two sessions, April natural gas futures inched higher in early Tuesday trading as markets sought clarity on the direction of weather trends and the war with Iran.

items.title: Oil Prices Tumble Back Below $100 on Ceasefire Optimism
items.summary: Oil prices fell sharply in early asian trade on Wednesday, with both major benchmarks dropping more than 5% as traders reacted to signs of potential de-escalation in the Middle East conflict as well as a crude inventory build in the U.S. At the time

items.title: Saudi Arabia’s Red Sea Export Route Is No Escape from Risk

items.title: Resource Wars Are Here and Oil Is the First Casualty
items.summary: In just over a year, the world saw several instances of a choked supply of commodities indispensable for today’s economies and military capabilities. From China’s restrictions on rare earths and critical minerals supply to the de facto closure of the

items.title: US Crude Oil, Product Inventories Rise
items.summary: The American Petroleum Institute (API) estimated that crude oil inventories in the United States rose by 2.3 million barrels in the week ending March 20. In the week prior, US crude oil inventories rose by 6.556 million barrels. Analysts had expected

items.title: Why Oil Majors Are Quietly Betting Big on Libya Again
